creator: eps+pbug0703@ana.com
text:
With one exception, everything I've ever written to run under
Tcl/Tk 8.3 contains conditionals to accommodate major differences
between 8.3 and 8.4.  This is that one exception, and there were
good reasons for it.  There is a web page, linked from the port
description, which quite explicitly states this software is not
intended to be used with any Tcl/Tk versions other than 8.3.3-
8.3.5, and that it will not work with 8.4.

This PR fails to account for fundamental changes between Tk 8.3
and Tk 8.4 (i.e. namespace migration), and, if committed in its
present form, I believe it would replace a working version with
one that will exhibit runtime failures.

Again, let me remind you there is absolutely no benefit to the
end-user from attempting to use the "wrong" Tcl/Tk version.  It
brings no new features, no improvements in usability, stability,
or security; it's just an untested, unsupported combination.

It's too bad bsd.port.mk/bsd.tcl.mk aren't sufficiently flexible
to allow something like this:

WANT_TK=	yes
.include <bsd.port.pre.mk>
.if $(HAVE_TK:M84}
CATEGORIES+=	tk84
USE_TK=		84
PATCHFILES=	patch-tk84-1.0.txt
PATCH_SITES=	http://tools.ana.com/demos/
.else
CATEGORIES+=	tk83
USE_TK=		83
.endif
.include <bsd.port.post.mk>

(The above snippet is fake.  The "vendor patch" is real.)

Comments on specific aspects of proposed changes:

1. Dropping USE_X_PREFIX: OK, consistent with the move away from
X11BASE to LOCALBASE

2. Replacing RUN_DEPENDS with USE_TK: OK

3. Replacing pkg-plist with PLIST_FILES: OK

4. Removing hard-coded device name and allowing packaging: I'm
not convinced depending on an environment variable is the best
way.  Perhaps there should be a $HOME/.sonytvrc and/or
${LOCALBASE}/etc/sonytvrc?  How about putting a
"link whatever sonytv" in /etc/devfs.conf and referencing
/dev/sonytv?

In any case, human-readable instructions in post-install should
be moved into a pkg-message file so they won't "disappear" once
a package is built.  Better still, pop up a tk_messageBox at run
time.

BTW, the best way to set an environment variable isn't with a
shell command, but via $HOME/.login_conf:

me:\
	:setenv=SONYTV=cuad0:

(/dev/ should be assumed if no absolute path is given.)

If you don't have any scripts, you don't need SCRIPTS_ENV.

5. Foreground/Background colors: These were chosen to represent a
specific theme, and tested for usability on several different
displays.  You're the first person ever to complain about them.

Please leave the defaults the way they are, and invent
appropriate knobs (or do something at runtime) if you feel the
original colors are inappropriate for your needs.  Don't ruin the
fun for everybody else.

-- Why this software is only supported with Tcl/Tk 8.3.x --

Debian Linux was one of the deployment platforms for this
software.  The Debian folks have a strong preference for stable
technology; they prefer backporting fixes to upgrading versions.
Tcl/Tk 8.3.3 was specified as target because this maximized
compatibility with the majority of systems in use at the time.
Unfortunately, it also meant that I couldn't make this 100%
"pure" Tcl/Tk, and had to exec /bin/stty instead of using
fconfigure -mode.  This limitation shouldn't apply on 8.4, except
that FreeBSD has been distributing bad 8.4 packages for a long
time.  :-(

-- Why Tcl 8.4.x package builds can't be trusted on FreeBSD --

If you see this in the configure stage of the build log:

	checking termios vs. termio vs. sgtty... none

instead of this:

	checking termios vs. termio vs. sgtty... termios

that means you built Tcl without serial channel support.  This
manifests as this run-time error:

bad option "-mode": should be one of -blocking, -buffering, -buffersize, -encoding, -eofchar, or -translation

-- How to tell if your Tcl 8.4.x is bad --

[broken]
$ tclsh8.4
% fconfigure [open /dev/cuad0 RDWR]
-blocking 1 -buffering full -buffersize 4096 -encoding iso8859-1 -eofchar {{} {}} -translation {auto lf}
% exit

[correct]
$ tclsh8.4
%  fconfigure [open /dev/cuad0 RDWR]
-blocking 1 -buffering full -buffersize 4096 -encoding iso8859-1 -eofchar {{} {}} -translation {auto crlf} -mode 9600,n,8,1 -xchar { }
% exit

(replace "cuad0" with any serial device file you have read/write access to)
